title: Module Check: PostgreSQL, RLS, and pgvector

questions.question: What two SQL statements are needed to enable Row Level Security on a table and why is the policy required in addition to enabling RLS?
questions.answer: `ALTER TABLE t ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;` enables the feature. `CREATE POLICY ... USING (...)` defines which rows each session is allowed to see. Without a policy, enabling RLS denies all access by default (no rows match). The policy provides the per-session workspace filter.

questions.question: Why must every vector search in Meridian include both `user_id = $1` AND `workspace_id = $2`?
questions.answer: user_id scopes results to the right person's memories. workspace_id enforces tenant isolation — without it a cross-workspace query could leak another tenant's documents. Either filter alone is insufficient: user_id alone allows cross-tenant leakage; workspace_id alone allows one user to see another's private memories.

questions.question: What does drizzle-zod provide and at which layer does it belong?
questions.answer: drizzle-zod auto-generates Zod validation schemas from Drizzle table definitions. It belongs at the API validation layer — the generated schema validates request bodies before any DB query, keeping the Zod schema in sync with the DB schema automatically.

questions.question: Which pgvector operator finds the nearest neighbours by cosine distance?
questions.explanation: `<=>` is cosine distance (most common for text embeddings). `<->` is L2 (Euclidean) distance. `<#>` is inner product (negative dot product — minimise for maximum similarity). `<~>` is not a pgvector operator.

questions.question: In Meridian's tenant middleware, where is the workspace_id sourced from, and why?

options.A: From req.body.workspace_id, to keep the API flexible
options.B: From the JWT payload, after the token is verified
options.C: From a query parameter, for REST compatibility
options.D: From the database session variable, set before each query

questions.explanation: workspace_id is sourced from the verified JWT — never from req.body or query params, which are attacker-controlled. The JWT is signed by the server, so workspace_id inside it cannot be forged. The middleware then sets `SET LOCAL app.current_workspace_id` from this verified value.
